next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 5

Two days since the outriders and Vel hadn't spent a point. Twenty-eight on the Ledger, enough if the Hoarded took their time, enough if Kem stopped reaching for levels every time the world asked for plain effort first.

By late afternoon his breathing had changed behind her. Yesterday's open-mouthed huffing had narrowed into careful pulls, each one worked around the cracked rib he'd carried out of the fight. The secondhand trader boots he'd gotten after abandoning the ruined corridor pair kept the stones off his blistered soles, but the left heel was rubbing him raw all over again. When the stream bent under a stand of hornbeam and gave her water, cover, and a strip of dry ground above the bank, she stopped.

"Here. Camp."

Kem let his pack slide down one shoulder at a time. Halfway through, the rib caught him. He froze with his teeth bared, waited out the white spike of it, then lowered the load to the ground so carefully it looked ceremonial.

"Sit," she said. Then, before he could, "No. Don't. If you lock up now, you'll stay locked."

"I'm not sure I have a vote."

"You don't. Fire. Food. Shelter. You're doing all of it."

His face went through disbelief, annoyance, and the tired acceptance she'd started trusting more than any promise. Getting from standing to a crouch took visible planning. So did standing back up. Good. The body would teach him faster than talk.

Vel dropped the fire kit onto the flat rock by the stream. The wrap held waxed cloth, striker, flint, char cloth, and a bundle of dry tinder she'd kept tucked under the pot. He looked down at it a...
open_hooks_to_carry: Kem's first real combat — power without technique
Vel's surgical efficiency vs. Kem's chaotic waste: 3 levels vs. 41 for comparable results
Kem's exhilaration — he has never felt his body work before

prev_tail_excerpt:
"Yes."

"Don't."

They started south.

His pace was bad from the beginning. He was too careful to be efficient and too stubborn to stop. Gravel worked into split skin and made him breathe in small measured pulls. He kept going anyway. Vel noted that and refused to make it kindness.

The road ran through mixed forest thick with spring growth. Warm air carried mud, green bark, rot from the feast behind them. By full dark they would have boots, a pack on his shoulders, maybe enough dried meat to get through the next stretch. By dawn the road would tip upward into the highlands and he would learn new parts of his body the hard way.

Beside her, Kem limped south with the Aldraste ring still on his hand and the cost of his education rubbing raw under every step.

The number stayed with her: two hundred and eighty-six. It was too high to waste and high enough to alter what the end of this road might ask of both of them. She could not afford his ignorance and could not fully cure it either.

He was going to hate her later. That much was certain.

Later could keep its place.

For now he was walking south when he could have turned back, and she was walking beside him when she could have left him to wear himself down alone. That was enough movement for one day.

"Vel?"

"What."

"Is every practical fact in the world delivered in that tone, or is it just me."

"Just you."

He kept talking as they walked, and she let him. The road went south. The sun dropped. Neither of them looked back.
